executive_summary.main_recommendation: AI spend appears productive overall but unevenly allocated: total spend is 81.7% of budget, forecast to decline 5.6% next month, while productivity gain is 17.5%. The priority is to reduce or cap spend for the clear overspenders and quality-risk users whose token usage materially exceeds their productivity, while replicating the workflows of high-ROI and efficient users. Low-adoption users should receive coaching rather than budget cuts, because their low usage suggests unrealized value rather than meaningful cost waste. Next month, watch whether story points shift toward the efficient-user cohort while total AI spend stays at or below the 73,284.79 USD forecast.

employee_metrics.name: Leila Park
employee_metrics.recommendation: Leila Park is the clearest model to replicate: rank #1/40 in story points (P100) and #2/40 in productivity (P98) while token usage is only P12, giving a positioning index of +85. This is high-value AI spend, not waste, because delivery is +202.2% vs median and productivity is +1951.3% vs median despite low relative usage. Replicate this workflow by documenting her prompt structure, context selection, and task sequencing as a team playbook. Next month, watch story points from teammates adopting her workflow.

employee_metrics.name: Amina Blanc
employee_metrics.recommendation: Amina Blanc is an efficient user with story point rank #14/40 (P68), productivity P90, and token usage P20, for a positioning index of +70. Her usage is well below heavier users, yet productivity is +582.4% vs median, which indicates a workflow worth scaling rather than constraining. Replicate her prompt discipline, context-selection habits, and task patterns across similar roles. Next month, watch productivity percentile for the peers who adopt her workflow.

Replicate his workflow by capturing his prompt templates and lightweight usage habits for broader training. Next month, watch token usage percentile for coached peers to see if they can reduce spend without losing delivery.
